title: betula nana subsp. rotundifolia
intro: Betula nana subsp. rotundifolia, also known as dwarf birch, is a subspecies of the Betula nana species, a small deciduous shrub native to the Arctic tundra. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and preventing infection. The health benefits of Betula nana subsp. rotundifolia include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by steeping the leaves or twigs in hot water or a solvent, and are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues.
